nl_proof: (1) The length of the red car is 3 meters.
(2) The length of the black car is 5 meters.
(3) The length of the red car is less than the length of the black car?
If some X equals ?:Y ?:Zs and some U equals ?:V ?:Zs and some V is less than some Y then U is less than X. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
(1) If some X equals ?:Y ?:Zs and some U equals ?:V ?:Zs and some V is less than some Y then U is less than X  [background knowledge]
(2) If some X equals ?:Y ?:Zs and some Y is less than some U then X is less than ?:U ?:Zs  [from steps 1]
(3) If some X is less than some Z then ?:X ?:Ys is less than ?:Z ?:Ys  [from steps 2]
(4) If the length of red car D is less than the length of black car Q then answer holds  [sentence 3]
(5) The length of red car D equals 3 meters  [sentence 1]
(6) The length of black car Q equals 5 meters  [sentence 2]
(7) If 3 meters is less than 5 meters then answer holds  [from steps 4, 5, 6]
(8) assume for contradiction: the answer is no  [from question]
(9) 3 Meters is not less than 5 meters  [from steps 7, 8]
